title: podman push fails : Error copying image to the remote destination...no such file or directory
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

podman build completes, then following podman push fails, both to docker or to a remote registry

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
cat > Dockerfile << EOF
FROM ubuntu:xenial
RUN apt-get update
EOF
podman build -t ubuntu:xenical-apt-update .
podman push ubuntu:xenical-apt-update docker-daemon:ubuntu:xenical-apt-update
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: Error copying image to the remote destination: Error writing blob: open /media/workspace-data/cameronbraid-local/share/containers/storage/overlay/0d0fbdb24d0b5d1d4783e953b3a549cb67152acc396f4ece9fc70165a6ad25a9/merged/var/lib/apt/lists/archive.ubuntu.com_ubuntu_dists_xenial-updates_InRelease: no such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Push should succeed

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.5.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```


**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.4
  podman version: 1.5.0
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.10.1
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.0, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "19.04"
  MemFree: 521469952
  MemTotal: 33401958400
  OCIRuntime:
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 10686296064
  SwapTotal: 10737414144
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: lion
  kernel: 5.0.0-25-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 1h 22m 15.46s (Approximately 0.04 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/cameronbraid/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 5
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /media/workspace-data/cameronbraid-local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 45
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
  VolumePath: /home/cameronbraid/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/disco,now 1.5.0-2~ubuntu19.04~ppa8 amd64 [installed]
podman/disco 1.5.0-2~ubuntu19.04~ppa8 i386
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

```
physical 

/etc/lsb-release
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=19.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=disco
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 19.04"

uname -a
Linux lion 5.0.0-25-generic #26-Ubuntu SMP Thu Aug 1 12:04:58 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

```

comments.body:
This works if you run podman as root

```
cat > Dockerfile << EOF
FROM ubuntu:xenial
RUN apt-get update
EOF
sudo podman build -t ubuntu:xenical-apt-update .
sudo podman push ubuntu:xenical-apt-update docker-daemon:ubuntu:xenical-apt-update
```

Hmm. And you have access to the Docker socket as the user you're running Podman as?

This seems to be coming out of c/image (`copy.Image()`). However, the missing file doesn't look like c/image code as much. @mtrmac Does this look like it might be a c/image bug, or would you suspect this is something about the interaction of build and push?
@vrothberg any ideas?
Was just looking at it. It doesn't look c/image related but the error is just surfacing there (reading from the source, writing to the destination).

Yep, its talking to docker just fine

```
cat > Dockerfile << EOF
FROM ubuntu:xenial
EOF
podman build -t ubuntu:xenical .
podman push ubuntu:xenical docker-daemon:ubuntu:xenical
```

```
cameronbraid@lion:/tmp/m$ cat > Dockerfile << EOF
> FROM ubuntu:xenial
> EOF
cameronbraid@lion:/tmp/m$ podman build -t ubuntu:xenical .

cameronbraid@lion:/tmp/m$ 
```
@cameronbraid, the error only occurs when pushing to a registry?
Occurs when pushing only, either to docker-daemon or a container registry like harbor
I am a bit confused. In the upper comment (https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3910#issuecomment-526609757), pushing to the daemon worked.
@cameronbraid, can you share the `--log-level=debug` logs when pushing to a registry?

That last comment uses a different Dockerfile - the difference being step 2 `RUN apt-get update` was removed to demonstrate that podman can talk to docker daemon fine

Which kind of file system is on `/media/workspace-data`?
/dev/mapper/ssd2-workspace--data on /media/workspace-data type ext4 (rw,relatime,errors=remount-ro)

@cameronbraid, thanks a lot for sharing the information! There's nothing jumping in my eye yet. Could you try doing the same but running podman as root? Please make sure to have the graph root on `/media/workspace-data` again. I want to figure out if it may be an issue with fuse-overlayfs. Speaking of which, could you also share the output of `fuse-overlayfs -V`?

@giuseppe @rhatdan @baude @mheon I think that we should add fuse-overlayfs and slirp4netns versions to podman info.
I agree. Seems to be fuse-overlay issue.

```
fuse-overlayfs: version 0.3
FUSE library version 3.4.1
using FUSE kernel interface version 7.27
fusermount3 version: 3.4.1
```

Not sure how to configure the graph root to be on another device.

That aside, when running as root podman works with the same Dockerfile, build and push.
Thanks a lot @cameronbraid! I think we need to update fuse-overlayfs in the Ubuntu ppa. The latest version is `v0.6.1`. @lsm5, can you have a look?

This is reporting an internal inconsistency in expanded images managed by c/storage. (IIRC that the recorded tarsplit metadata refers to files which are not present on the actual filesystem.)

So, root/non-root may not be the actual difference; it could well be “already corrupted store / not an already corrupted store”.

If you search for `no such file or directory` in the containers/* repos, there have been quite a few reports over time, and IIRC none of the ones I’ve seen have ever been traced down to a _definitive_ root cause.

It would be _very_ interesting to have a definitive reproducer; sadly, IIRC again, in most cases removing the local storage, and doing the same steps that demonstrate the failure succeeds (i.e. we don’t have a reproducer for _creating_ the corruption.)
What do I delete to `remove local storage` - my graphroot ?

```
# podman info | grep GraphRoot
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
$ podman info | grep GraphRoot
  GraphRoot: /home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers/storage
```
I removed the graph root and retried and am getting the same issue.

Its not just that file that is missing `/media/workspace-data/cameronbraid-local/share/containers/storage/overlay/67c727ee8a587390171c8f8be3206e6cde844819265f1542b822893b52e8626d/merged/var/lib/apt/lists/archive.ubuntu.com_ubuntu_dists_xenial_InRelease` but the merged folder is missing: `/media/workspace-data/cameronbraid-local/share/containers/storage/overlay/67c727ee8a587390171c8f8be3206e6cde844819265f1542b822893b52e8626d/merged`
What happens if you do a podman mount of the container, can you see the content?

does this help :

```
> podman images

localhost/ubuntu           xenical-apt-update   13f320789979   43 minutes ago   150 MB

stat: cannot stat '/var/lib/apt/lists/archive.ubuntu.com_ubuntu_dists_xenial_InRelease': No such file or directory

> podman run -it 13f320789979 ls -l /var/lib/apt/lists | grep archive.ubuntu.com_ubuntu_dists_xenial_InRelease
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root   246846 Apr 21  2016 archive.ubuntu.com_ubuntu_dists_xenial_InRelease

```

> Thanks a lot @cameronbraid! I think we need to update fuse-overlayfs in the Ubuntu ppa. The latest version is `v0.6.1`. @lsm5, can you have a look?

indeed.  Let's first try with an updated version of fuse-overlayfs.  0.3 is really old (released in January) and a lot of changes went in since then.

Some good news, fuse-overlayfs 0.6.1 fixes it

```
> fuse-overlayfs --version
fuse-overlayfs: version 0.6.1
FUSE library version 3.6.2
using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
fusermount3 version: 3.4.1
>

Getting image source signatures

Copying blob aeda103e78c9 done
Copying blob e79142719515 done
Copying blob 7b294dafdae5 done
Copying config 13f3207899 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```
Would be good to get the ubuntu PPA up to date.  I just built using podman and coppied the binary out of the container into my host
